title: Add the risk item kind
description:
Add `risk` as a markdown-first item kind so risks authored in issue descriptions become addressable, queryable items. Configure the kind in `[item_kinds]` (its section, id-pattern, marker, and the `mitigates:`/`resolves:` link namespaces) and index risks from descriptions via the generic triple-driven parse path the `requirement` kind already uses. The markdown description stays the sole source of truth, with no duplicated structured store. Builds on scoped-id addressing and the six-tuple kind registry.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: `jit item list --kind risk` returns risk items parsed from issue descriptions.
- [hard] REQ-02: A `mitigates:<qualified-id>` label and a `resolves:<qualified-id>` label each resolve to the addressed risk item.
- [hard] REQ-03: The markdown description is the only source for risk items; no parallel structured store duplicates them.
- [hard] REQ-04: The behavior is covered by `cargo test`.
